original_abstract: Accurate citywide traffic flow prediction is fundamental to the advancement of intelligent transportation systems. Despite significant progress in modeling complex spatio-temporal correlations, existing methods frequently overlook two critical challenges: spatial heterogeneity, where diverse urban regions exhibit vastly different flow distributions, and temporal heterogeneity, where traffic patterns shift dynamically over time. Traditional models typically rely on shared parameter spaces that fail to account for these localized and time-varying nuances. To address these limitations, we introduce the Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL) framework. ST-SSL integrates spatial and temporal convolutions to encode multi-dimensional information, enhanced by a novel self-supervised learning paradigm designed to capture heterogeneity. Specifically, our approach employs adaptive data augmentation at both the attribute and structural levels of the traffic graph. By constructing two auxiliary SSL tasks atop this augmented data, the framework sharpens its representation of idiosyncratic spatial and temporal patterns. Empirical evaluations across four benchmark datasets demonstrate that ST-SSL consistently outperforms state-of-the-art baselines. Given the prevalence of spatio-temporal heterogeneity in real-world data, this framework offers a robust solution for a wide range of urban computing applications. Our implementation is publicly available at https://github.com/Echo-Ji/ST-SSL.
humanized_abstract: Accurate citywide traffic flow prediction is a crucial element of intelligent transportation systems (ITS). While many existing works focus on exploring and modeling the underlying complex spatio-temporal correlations and achieving promising performance, there still remain two major and non-trivial challenges to be addressed in the existing works: 1) spatial heterogeneity, i.e., the varying distribution of traffic flow in different urban areas, and 2) temporal heterogeneity, i.e., the dynamic changes of traffic patterns over time. Most existing works are based on shared parameter space, and are hard to fully model these unique local and time-varying characteristics. To deal with these challenges, in this paper, we propose a novel Spatio-Temporal Self-Supervised Learning (ST-SSL) framework for citywide traffic flow prediction. The proposed ST-SSL framework first explores the underlying spatial and temporal features of traffic flow by using spatial and temporal convolution, while the second key component is the self-supervised learning (SSL) technique, which is adopted to explore the heterogeneity. In particular, the adaptive attribute data augmentation and structural data augmentation are introduced at the traffic graph level, and two auxiliary self-supervised learning (SSL) tasks are constructed upon the augmented data to guide the network to learn more distinguishable spatial and temporal characteristics for traffic flow prediction. The experimental results on four large-scale real-world citywide traffic datasets demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed ST-SSL framework against several state-of-the-art traffic prediction methods. The proposed framework is widely applicable in many urban computing systems that often encounter heterogeneous urban areas and dynamic traffic patterns. Our implementation is available online at https://github.com/Echo-Ji/ST-SSL.
